summary: Choose the right mix of passive and active scanning for the target.
examples: Run passive scanning during a manual walkthrough.
Schedule an active scan during a test window.
Use AJAX spider + active scan for SPAs.
content:
## Overview
Passive scanning analyzes observed traffic without changing requests. Active scanning sends attack payloads to find vulnerabilities.

### Use passive scanning when
- You are exploring the app manually
- You need a safe baseline scan

### Use active scanning when
- You have explicit permission
- You can run in a maintenance window

### Combine for best coverage
Start with passive scans and follow up with targeted active scans.
title: Guide 5: Passive vs Active Scanning
steps: Explore the app through the proxy to generate traffic.
Review passive alerts and fix obvious issues first.
Define scope to prevent out-of-scope scans.
Run a targeted active scan on key areas.
